title: In-process scoped subagents (spawn_subagent tool) + opt-in MCP tool bridge
description: The last two capability areas, both opt-in. (1) Subagents: AgenticLoopParams.subagents registers a spawn_subagent tool; its handler runs a nested runAgenticLoop with a fresh context, a scoped tool subset, optional per-agent model/effort/maxTurns, charging the SAME parent Budget, and returns the child's finalText to the parent as a tool result — the parent's context grows by the summary only. (2) MCP bridge: an off-by-default config axis exposes a configured MCP server's tools as loop ToolDefs routed through a bridge using the repo's existing MCP client transport; disabled (default) spawns nothing and changes nothing, preserving loop hermeticity.

successCriteria.description: With subagents configured, the parent loop exposes a spawn_subagent tool; invoking it runs a nested loop whose client sees ONLY the subagent's scoped tools and a fresh message history (no parent turns), and the child's finalText comes back as the parent's tool result.
The nested loop honors per-agent model, effort and maxTurns overrides and charges tokens/costUsd into the parent's Budget instance (combined spend visible; a child cannot out-spend a parent ceiling).
A child that refuses, exceeds budget, or errors surfaces as an isError tool result to the parent (loop continues; parent decides), never a throw.

Without a subagents param the spawn_subagent tool is not registered and the tool list is byte-identical; with the MCP bridge disabled (default) no MCP process/transport is created and the tool list is unchanged.

With the bridge enabled in config, the configured MCP server's tools appear as ToolDefs (unmarked readOnly → serial execution), calls route through the bridge to the MCP transport, and close() tears the connection down cleanly at loop end.
npm run build and npm run typecheck pass.

nonGoals: Do not allow recursive subagent nesting deeper than one level (child loops get no subagents param — hard cap, mirrors the Workflow one-level rule).
Do not register the MCP bridge on the hermetic claude-code child path or fleet children (--strict-mcp-config stays).
Do not mark bridged MCP tools readOnly (unknown side effects → serial per ADR-2's conservative default).
Do not build persona-file (.md) loading into subagent defs — SubagentDef is a programmatic surface; the existing agent-loader personas remain the role layer.
stopConditions: npm run build passes and tests assert: scoped fresh-context spawn with summary-only return, shared-Budget accounting with overrides, fail-soft child outcomes, double off-by-default (no tool, no MCP spawn), and bridged round-trip with clean close.
definitionOfDone: A parent loop can delegate a bounded subtask to a fresh-context nested loop with scoped tools and per-agent model/effort under the parent's budget, and a config-enabled MCP bridge can extend the tool catalog through the existing MCP client — while both features absent leave every byte of current behavior unchanged. Build and full suite green.
assumptions: runAgenticLoop is re-entrant (pure function over its params) so nesting is safe; the child gets its own params object and shares only the Budget reference.
The repo already has an MCP client transport (src/graph/client.ts MCP initialize + tools/call pattern, fixed 2026-06-20) the bridge can reuse or mirror — no new MCP dependency.

The bridge config lives as an optional section (e.g. config.tools.mcpBridge { enabled: false, server: { command, args } }) following the egress-axis default-off idiom used by medical/research sections.
SubagentDef = { name, description, systemPrompt, tools: string[], model?, effort?, maxTurns? } — an own type; per-agent effort rides ChatParams.effort from sprint 3 (hard dependsOn).
spawn_subagent's input schema is { name: string, task: string } — the model picks a configured subagent by name and passes the task text.
outOfScope: Parallel subagent fan-out (one spawn per tool call; the fleet layer owns bulk fan-out).
MCP server lifecycle management beyond start/close for a single configured server.
Exposing subagents or the bridge in bober.config.json role sections for pipeline roles (a consumer wires them explicitly; config surface here is the bridge axis only).
generatorNotes: Subagents: create src/orchestrator/subagents.ts exporting SubagentDef and buildSubagentTool(defs, parentParams): { tool: ToolDef, handler: ToolHandler }. The handler: resolve def by name (unknown name → isError result listing valid names); build child AgenticLoopParams { client: parentParams.client (or a model-overridden client via the provider factory when def.model set — reuse how roles resolve clients), systemPrompt: def.systemPrompt, userMessage: task, tools: parentParams.tools filtered to def.tools, toolHandlers filtered likewise, maxTurns: def.maxTurns ?? 10, effort: def.effort, budget: parentParams.budget (SAME instance), subagents: undefined }; await runAgenticLoop; map result → tool result text (finalText, or isError for refused/budget_exceeded/error with the stopReason named). In agentic-loop.ts (or the entry assembling tools), when params.subagents?.length, append the built tool/handler before the loop starts. MCP bridge: create src/orchestrator/tools/mcp-bridge.ts exporting createMcpToolBridge(server: {command, args}): Promise<{ tools: ToolDef[], handlers: Map<string,ToolHandler>, close(): Promise<void> }> — initialize over stdio using the same MCP handshake/tool-call encoding as src/graph/client.ts (tools/list → ToolDefs with a 'mcp__' name prefix to avoid collisions; tools/call → result text). Config: optional ToolsSectionSchema { mcpBridge?: { enabled: z.boolean().default(false), server: {...} } } — but ONLY construct the bridge when enabled === true at the call site (never at config parse). Ensure close() runs in a finally at loop end. Inject the transport for tests (accept an optional transport factory) so no real process spawn is needed.
evaluatorNotes: sc-10-1: parent fake client scripts a spawn_subagent tool call; child fake client (or shared scripted client distinguishing by system prompt) asserts the child's first request contains ONLY scoped tool defs and no parent messages; parent's next turn receives the child finalText as tool result. sc-10-2: shared Budget — assert usdSpent/tokensSpent include child turns; set a ceiling the child crosses → child stops with budget_exceeded and parent sees isError naming it (also covers sc-10-3 partially). sc-10-3: child scripted refusal → parent isError mentioning refusal; loop continues. sc-10-4: no subagents → tool defs deep-equal baseline; default config → assert injected transport factory never called. sc-10-5: stub transport implementing initialize/tools-list/tools-call; assert bridged ToolDefs appear prefixed, a call round-trips, readOnly is absent on them, and close() called once at loop end (finally). Build + typecheck + FULL suite.
